page: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 Intel Xeon 8462Y+ vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz with an x86_64 architecture, 24 physical cores, and a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, providing 20.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, delivering a combined 160 GB of VRAM. This configuration does not include local storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 55980.84 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 52357.03. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4086.55 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 110.72 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform in the top tier at 35.53 GB/sec and 15.63 GB/sec respectively. For AI workloads, LLM inference on a small 135M model demonstrates top-tier performance, processing prompts at 10457.59 tokens/sec and generating text at 366.07 tokens/sec.

The server's hardware profile offers high resource density, combining massive GPU memory with substantial system RAM, making it highly cost-efficient for specialized parallel computing tasks. However, the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs that require external storage solutions. This server is optimized for large-scale AI and machine learning workloads, LLM inference, data science jobs, and high-throughput database operations.
description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 Intel Xeon 8462Y+ vCPUs, 480.0 GB of RAM, and two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 160 GB of total VRAM. Built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this server does not include local storage. It delivers strong performance across benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 55980.84 and top-tier LLM inference speeds of 10457.59 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4086.55 GB/sec for cached reads. While the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage are notable tradeoffs, the high density of GPU and system memory makes this configuration highly cost-efficient for parallel computing. It is best suited for machine learning, LLM inference, data science, and database operations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server with 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs providing 160 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and LLM inference.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a KVM-based server featuring 24 vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 160 GB VRAM.
tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring dual NVIDIA Hopper H100 accelerators and 480 GB RAM for demanding machine learning and data science workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM
24 Intel Xeon 8462Y+ vCPUs with 480 GB system memory
Top-tier LLM inference performance for prompt processing and text generation
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput database operations
Optimized for machine learning, data science, and parallel computing workloads
